medications.drug_name: antifungal — single oral fluconazole OR topical clotrimazole
medications.indication_rationale: First-line for candidiasis; source used fluconazole 150mg orally stat plus clotrimazole 1% cream for symptomatic relief. IMPORTANT: avoid oral fluconazole in pregnancy — use a topical imidazole instead; confirm pregnancy status.
medications.dose_route_frequency: fluconazole 150mg orally as a single dose (non-pregnant), OR clotrimazole pessary/cream course; clotrimazole 1% cream to the vulva twice daily for symptoms (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Avoid oral fluconazole in pregnancy (teratogenicity risk) — use a topical imidazole; confirm pregnancy status before prescribing.
Scented products and douching irritate and disturb the vaginal flora — avoid.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wear loose cotton underwear, avoid scented soaps/'feminine hygiene' sprays and douching, and wash with water only; avoid intercourse until settled.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces moisture/irritation and restores the natural balance.

patient_education_points.point: This is vaginal thrush (a yeast infection), commonly triggered by antibiotics upsetting the normal balance. An antifungal (a tablet or a cream/pessary) clears it — itch and burning usually improve within 24–48 hours.

Wear cotton underwear, wash with water only (no scented soaps, sprays or douching), and avoid intercourse until it settles. If you could be pregnant, let us know — you'd use the cream/pessary rather than the tablet.

See a doctor / go to urgent care if you develop a fever, deep pelvic or lower-abdominal pain, abnormal bleeding, or an offensive/frothy discharge — that suggests a different or deeper infection. Also see your GP if this keeps recurring (four or more times a year).

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response; swab/STI screen if atypical; investigate recurrence (e.g. diabetes); pregnancy-appropriate treatment.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: PID/STI features — urgent review; possible pregnancy — GP for topical treatment.

must_include_items: Recognise candidiasis (thick white non-malodorous discharge, itch, post-antibiotic) vs BV/trichomonas/STI
Antifungal (oral fluconazole OR topical) — confirm pregnancy status (avoid oral fluconazole in pregnancy)
STI/PID safety-net (fever, deep pelvic pain, abnormal bleeding, offensive/frothy discharge)
acceptable_alternatives: Single oral fluconazole OR a topical imidazole (pessary/cream); longer regimen for recurrent disease
GP follow-up; swab/STI screen if atypical
errors_of_omission: Not checking pregnancy status / drug suitability
errors_of_commission: Oral fluconazole in a possibly-pregnant patient without checking
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires an antifungal with a pregnancy/drug-suitability check, an STI/PID safety-net, and irritant-avoidance advice.
